description: Formulas and examples for conduit fill, voltage drop, motor starting, 4-20mA scaling, cable tray fill

conduit_fill.formula: Total conductor area / conduit internal area x 100 = fill %
conduit_fill.tip: Stay under 35% for long runs or runs with many bends — just because it fits by math doesn't mean pulling is easy

voltage_drop.single_phase: Vd = (2 x K x I x L) / CM
voltage_drop.three_phase: Vd = (1.732 x K x I x L) / CM
voltage_drop.example: 480V 3-phase, 50A, 400ft, #6 AWG copper (26240 CM): Vd = 1.732 x 12.9 x 50 x 400 / 26240 = 17V = 3.55% — marginal, consider upsizing to #4 AWG (2.23%)

motor_starting_current.breaker_sizing_NEC_430_52: Inverse time: 250% x FLA, round up to next standard size
motor_starting_current.VFD_note: VFDs eliminate high inrush — starting current typically 100-150% FLA

cable_tray_fill.multiconductor_cables: Sum of cross-sectional areas must not exceed tray allowable fill (24-inch tray = 28 sq-in)
cable_tray_fill.signal_control: Fill depth not to exceed 50% of tray rail height
cable_tray_fill.weight_check: Calculate total cable weight per foot + tray weight, verify against manufacturer load tables at support span

heat_tracing_load.add_for_fittings: 2 ft trace per valve, 1 ft per flange pair, 3 ft per instrument connection
heat_tracing_load.safety_factor: Add 10-20% for aging, wet insulation, wind
heat_tracing_load.common_mistake: Not accounting for valves/flanges/instruments in total trace length — these are significant heat sinks
